newsBriefing: Spring training headlines across Major League Baseball are being dominated by World Baseball Classic storylines, injuries and roster shakeups. The biggest immediate blow came when Astros All-Star shortstop Jeremy Peña fractured the tip of his right ring finger during a WBC exhibition game, an injury expected to sideline him for at least the start of the tournament and potentially threaten his Opening Day availability. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0} Meanwhile the WBC itself is gathering momentum: Team USA opened preparations with veteran ace Clayton Kershaw receiving a warm reception in Colorado while analysts widely list the United States as the tournament favorite, with Japan viewed as the primary challenger for the title. Around the league, several roster and health situations are developing as camps intensify. Cincinnati ace Hunter Greene is undergoing an MRI after experiencing stiffness in his right elbow, creating early concern for the Reds’ rotation. Texas is adding veteran depth with reports that former Pirates star Andrew McCutchen has agreed to join the Rangers organization. Atlanta is also adjusting after losing Jurickson Profar to suspension, with the Braves acknowledging someone in the lineup will need to step forward. On the international front, roster uncertainty continues to ripple through WBC teams: Mets prospect Christian McLean’s arrival to the tournament has been delayed by vertigo symptoms, while Phillies center fielder Johan Rojas is reportedly facing a potential 80-game suspension after testing positive for a performance-enhancing substance, which has already removed him from the Dominican Republic’s WBC plans. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1} With the WBC about to begin and Opening Day just weeks away, the league’s biggest stories right now sit at the intersection of international competition, spring-training injuries and late roster moves that could reshape early-season expectations.
